meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't collaborate. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't collaborate
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome collaboration bottlenecks. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for fast delivery.
content.answer_block: Collaboration bottlenecks destroy freelance margins. By adopting a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you eliminate repetitive setup and standardize your codebase. Using AI-driven tools like Antigravity and Claude Code allows you to synchronize architectural patterns instantly, ensuring your projects are scalable, maintainable, and ready for multi-developer handoffs from day one.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your project deadline, but you're stuck in a 'collaboration hell'—files are scattered, environments are misconfigured, and your client is losing faith. This isn't just a technical hurdle; it's a structural failure that drains your billable hours and kills your reputation as a high-velocity developer.

It’s time to stop reinventing the wheel. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from a chaotic solo endeavor into a professional-grade production pipeline. With Antigravity and Claude Code, you bridge the gap between initial concept and collaborative reality, allowing you to ship features that don't just work, but scale.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize a standardized SaaS starter kit to ensure all foundational dependencies and folder structures are consistent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready SaaS starter kit with Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase integration. Focus on modular folder structures for collaborative scalability.

steps.title: Configure Collaborative Auth
steps.description: Implement robust authentication that supports role-based access control (RBAC) to allow future team members to work safely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up Supabase Auth with RBAC middleware. Create a 'roles' table and helper functions for admin, editor, and viewer access levels.

steps.title: Automate Type Definitions
steps.description: Sync database schemas to TypeScript types to prevent runtime errors when multiple developers touch the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate TypeScript types from my current Supabase schema and store them in a shared /types directory for global access.

steps.title: Standardize Linting and Formatting
steps.description: Enforce strict coding standards so that the codebase remains readable regardless of who is committing the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure ESLint and Prettier for strict Airbnb style guide compliance with automated Husky git hooks.

steps.title: Deploy CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Automate the testing and deployment process to ensure code quality before it hits production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Action workflow that runs unit tests and build checks on every pull request to main.

pro_tips: Always use environment variable templates (.env.example) to avoid leaking secrets when sharing code with clients.
Implement a 'Feature Flag' system early so you can toggle experimental code without breaking the main production branch.
Use a dedicated 'Documentation' folder inside your starter kit to keep track of API endpoints for non-technical client stakeholders.
Standardize your git commit messages using Conventional Commits; it makes reviewing history 10x faster for collaborators.
Build 'mock-data' factories into your starter kit to allow UI development to proceed before the backend is fully finished.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is deployed, invite your first collaborator to the repository and run a dry-run feature pull request to test the CI/CD pipeline.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with fragmented workflows and start building like a senior architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
